---
title: "Preface"
---

# About This Book {.unnumbered}

This ebook is a scholarly recreation and extension of a Master's thesis completed
at George Washington University in 2002, under the joint supervision of Dr. Jaroslaw
Sobieszczanski-Sobieski at NASA Langley Research Center. The original report was
published as NASA Contractor Report NASA/CR-2002-211652 [@altus2002blissrs].

The thesis introduced a response-surface-based variant of the *Bi-Level Integrated
System Synthesis* (BLISS) multidisciplinary design optimization framework. Where
earlier BLISS implementations relied on gradient-guided sensitivity derivatives to
couple subsystem and system-level optimizations, BLISS-RS replaces those derivatives
with quadratic response surface (RS) surrogate models. The result is an autonomous,
decomposition-based MDO algorithm well-suited to parallel and distributed computing
environments.

## What This Book Adds

This recreation does more than transcribe the original work. Each chapter includes:

- **Executable Julia code** — all algorithms are implemented from scratch in
  modern Julia, a high-performance scientific computing language. Readers can
  run, modify, and extend every example directly.
- **Interactive visualizations** — optimization histories, response surface plots,
  and design space explorations are rendered as publication-quality figures.
- **Modern extensions** (Chapter 9) — Gaussian process surrogates (Kriging),
  Bayesian optimization, and Julia's native parallelism are discussed as natural
  evolutions of the BLISS-RS framework.
- **SRM application** (Chapter 10) — a complete new application of BLISS-RS to the
  conceptual design of a solid propellant rocket motor, bridging the aerospace
  structures / aerodynamics context of the original thesis to the propulsion domain.

## Original Test Case

The benchmark problem throughout the original thesis — and retained here — is the
conceptual aerodynamic design of a **supersonic business jet** originally posed for
the 1995 AIAA Graduate Student Design Competition. Four engineering disciplines are
modeled as black-box analysis modules:

| Black Box | Analysis | Key Outputs |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| Structures | Equivalent Laminated Plate Structures (ELAPS) | Wing weight, aeroelastic twist |
| Aerodynamics | Wave-drag model (AWAVE) + drag polar | Lift, drag, $L/D$ |
| Power | Engine deck approximation | SFC, engine weight, ESF |
| Performance | Breguet range equation | Range |

BLISS-RS couples these four disciplines through a hierarchy of system-level and
local-level optimizations mediated by quadratic response surface surrogates.

## How to Use This Book

Each chapter is self-contained. Julia code blocks illustrate the method being
discussed and can be executed independently. The `src/` directory contains shared
Julia modules — `bliss_rs.jl`, `supersonic_jet.jl`, and `srm.jl` — that are
included at the start of each computational chapter.

To reproduce all results locally:

```bash
# 1. Install dependencies (requires pixi)
pixi run setup

# 2. Render to HTML
pixi run render

# 3. Live preview
pixi run preview
```

## Notation Conventions

| Symbol | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| $Z$ | System-level design variables (shared across disciplines) |
| $X$ | Local design variables (private to a black box) |
| $Y^*$ | Coupling variables (outputs of one BB used as inputs to another) |
| $Y^A$ | Actual coupling variable outputs from BB analysis |
| $w$ | Weighting factors for the synthetic local objective function |
| BB | Black box (disciplinary analysis module) |
| RS | Response surface (quadratic surrogate) |
| DOE | Design of experiments (point placement scheme) |
| $N_X$ | Number of design variables |
| $N_S$ | Number of sample points |

## Citation

If you use material from this work, please cite the original NASA contractor report:

> Altus, T. D., "A Response Surface Methodology for Bi-Level Integrated System
> Synthesis (BLISS)," NASA/CR-2002-211652, George Washington University /
> NASA Langley Research Center, Hampton, VA, May 2002.

---

*Troy David Altus*
*Elkton, Maryland*
